resources.string: Adding tenten and maru to make foreign sounds.

Good news! The "upgrade" system you learned for Hiragana works exactly the same way here! \n\nJust like before, adding two small strokes (tenten) or a tiny bubble (maru) changes the sound. Since Katakana is mostly for foreign loanwords, these "voiced" sounds are everywhere from food brands to tech terms!

Let's refresh your memory with these tricks: 

- K becomes G 
- S becomes Z 
- T becomes D 
- H becomes B 
- H becomes P


Remember that the same rule applies here like in the Hiragana lesson. 
That little maru bubble. Just remember that bubbles "pop" as in the letter "p"

The G-Row (Heavy K sounds) 
ガ ga | ギ gi | グ gu | ゲ ge | ゴ go 

English Example: Gasu (ガス) - Gas

The Z-Row (Heavy S sounds) 
ザ za | ジ ji | ズ zu | ゼ ze | ゾ zo 

English Example: Jūsu (ジュース) - Juice

The D-Row (Heavy T sounds) 
ダ da | ヂ ji | ヅ zu | デ de | ド do 

English Example: Dansu (ダンス) - Dance

The B-Row (Heavy H sounds) 
バ ba | ビ bi | ブ bu | ベ be | ボ bo 

English Example: Baggu (バッグ) - Bag

The P-Row (H Row + Maru Bubble) 
パ pa | ピ pi | プ pu | ペ pe | ポ po 

English Example: Pajama (パジャマ) - Pajamas
